title: Primary happy path
implementation_summary: Added the control-plane Claude review Primary happy path slice for RP00.P02.M04.S07.

The S07 policy, result-field registry, prechecker module, service function, validator, synthetic fixture, contract definition, README, and RP00 validator bind a frozen metadata-only primary happy path result after RP00.P02.M04.S06.

The precheck consumes the S06 Audit hint precheck result and requires the S01 service entrypoint contract, S02 normalized request, S03 tenant boundary precheck, S04 Matter trace precheck, S05 Permission precheck, S06 Audit hint precheck, RP00.P02.M03.S20 Hermes Validation denied-path evidence refs, and the permission/audit baseline RP00.P00.M05.S01 to remain intact.

The allowed path emits an allow_metadata_only happy path receipt only when the carried S06 audit hint decision is allow_metadata_only, audit_event_ref is satisfied, audit_hint is present, and a nonblank audit_event_ref remains present from the validated upstream request chain.

Missing S06 results, denied audit hint results, wrong upstream markers, non-Claude-review entrypoints, wrong model or effort, non-read-only review metadata, missing audit_hint, missing audit_event_ref, happy path bypass claims, audit ledger/event write claims, runtime Claude/review queue claims, runtime permission/AuthZ/permission-engine claims, review-gate bypass claims, unknown truthy happy path claims, and source-completion claims fail closed.

The S07 result hard-codes no audit ledger append, no audit event write, no runtime Claude invocation, no review queue write, no review assignment write, no review notification, no service logic execution, no runtime route creation, no runtime permission evaluation, no AuthZ evaluator call, no permission engine execution, no product-state write, no database write, and no storage write.

The dedicated service tests prove policy binding, metadata-only primary happy path receipt emission, firm-level metadata behavior, result drift rejection, unsafe-claim and wrong-source failures, prechecker no-write boundaries, and synthetic fixture parity.

The RP00 validator now enforces S07 contract definition, policy module, service module, validators, fixture metadata, weighted-ledger presence, and construction inspection flag control_plane_claude_review_primary_happy_path_verified.

The boundary is metadata-only and does not append audit ledgers, write audit events, invoke Claude at runtime, mutate review queues, replace human approval, write storage, write databases, or persist product state.
RP00.P02.M04 remains open after S07 and hands off to RP00.P02.M04.S08 Secondary workflow path; RP00.P02 and RP00 remain open.
